2604.09923.title: GLEaN: A Text-to-image Bias Detection Approach for Public Comprehension
2604.09923.paper_summary: Text-to-image (T2I) models, and their encoded biases, increasingly shape the visual media the public encounters. While researchers have produced a rich body of work on bias measurement, auditing, and mitigation in T2I systems, those methods largely target technical stakeholders, leaving a gap in public legibility. We introduce GLEaN (Generative Likeness Evaluation at N-Scale), a portrait-based explainability pipeline designed to make T2I model biases visually understandable to a broad audience. GLEaN comprises three stages: automated large-scale image generation from identity prompts, facial landmark-based filtering and spatial alignment, and median-pixel composition that distills a model's central tendency into a single representative portrait. The resulting composites require no statistical background to interpret; a viewer can see, at a glance, who a model 'imagines' when prompted with 'a doctor' versus a 'felon.' We demonstrate GLEaN on Stable Diffusion XL across 40 social and occupational identity prompts, producing composites that reproduce documented biases and surface new associations between skin tone and predicted emotion. We find in a between-subjects user study (N = 291) that GLEaN portraits communicate biases as effectively as conventional data tables, but require significantly less viewing time. Because the method relies solely on generated outputs, it can also be replicated on any black-box and closed-weight systems without access to model internals. GLEaN offers a scalable, model-agnostic approach to bias explainability, purpose-built for public comprehension, and is publicly available at https://github.com/cultureiolab/GLEaN.
2604.09923.paper_summary_zh: 文本到圖像（T2I）模型及其編碼的偏見，越來越多地影響著公眾所接觸的視覺媒體。雖然研究人員已經產出了一系列關於T2I系統中偏見測量、審計和緩解的豐富研究，但這些方法主要針對技術利益相關者，留下了公眾可讀性方面的空白。我們介紹了GLEaN（N尺度生成相似性評估），這是一個基於肖像的可解釋性管道，旨在使T2I模型的偏見對廣泛受眾可視化理解。GLEaN包括三個階段：從身份提示自動生成大規模圖像、基於面部標誌的過濾和空間對齊，以及中位像素組合，將模型的中心傾向提煉為單一代表性肖像。生成的合成圖像不需要統計背景即可解釋；觀眾可以一目了然地看到，當提示「一位醫生」與「一名罪犯」時，模型「想像」的是誰。我們在穩定擴散XL上展示了GLEaN，涵蓋40個社會和職業身份提示，生成的合成圖像再現了已記錄的偏見，並揭示了膚色與預測情感之間的新聯繫。我們在一項受試者之間的用戶研究中（N = 291）發現，GLEaN肖像以與傳統數據表同樣有效的方式傳達偏見，但所需的觀看時間顯著較少。由於該方法僅依賴生成的輸出，因此也可以在任何黑箱和封閉權重系統上復現，而無需訪問模型內部。GLEaN提供了一種可擴展的、與模型無關的偏見可解釋性方法，專為公眾理解而設計，並在https://github.com/cultureiolab/GLEaN上公開提供。
2604.09923.author: Bochu Ding et.al.
2604.09923.authors: Bochu Ding, Brinnae Bent, Augustus Wendell
